# Security Policy

## Scope

This repository hosts pre-built LCP manifest files (`.lcp.json.gz`) and a static browsing site. Security concerns in scope include:

- **Malicious or tampered manifests** — files that do not faithfully represent the scanned package
- **Vulnerabilities in the static site** — XSS or other client-side issues in `site/`
- **Issues in automation workflows** — unsafe code execution in `.github/workflows/`

Out of scope: vulnerabilities in the packages described by the manifests themselves (report those to the respective package maintainers).

## Reporting a Vulnerability

Please **do not open a public issue** for security concerns. Instead, use [GitHub's private vulnerability reporting](https://github.com/zazza123/lcp-registry/security/advisories/new) to reach the maintainers confidentially.

Include:
- A description of the issue
- The affected file(s) or manifest(s)
- Steps to reproduce or verify the problem

We aim to acknowledge reports within **48 hours** and resolve confirmed issues within **7 days**.

## Manifest Integrity

All manifests are generated with the [lcp](https://github.com/zazza123/lcp) SDK and can be independently verified:

```bash
lcp validate manifests/<language>/<letter>/<package>/<version>.lcp.json.gz
```

Manifests added via pull request are reviewed before merging. Automated manifests are generated by the weekly GitHub Actions workflow using pinned dependency versions.
